---
name: SEO Audit
description: Review public-facing pages for heading hierarchy, semantic structure, metadata quality, internal linking opportunities, and SEO-related content clarity in the Dermatologika project.
---

# SEO Audit Skill

Use this skill when reviewing or improving public-facing pages for SEO, semantic structure, metadata, and content organization.

This skill is for focused execution, not broad architectural planning.

## Goal

Ensure that a public page is:

- semantically clear
- easy to understand for users and search engines
- structured with correct heading hierarchy
- aligned with search intent and conversion intent
- free from obvious SEO structure issues

## Check These Areas

### 1. Heading Hierarchy

Verify:

- there is only one clear H1
- H2 and H3 follow real section hierarchy
- headings are not used only for styling
- headings are descriptive and specific

### 2. Semantic Structure

Verify:

- sections are meaningful
- semantic HTML is preferred where relevant
- content blocks are logically grouped
- page structure is easy to scan

### 3. Metadata

Check whether the page has:

- a clear title
- a useful meta description
- canonical logic where relevant
- open graph support where relevant
- structured data opportunities where relevant

### 4. Content Clarity

Review whether:

- the page matches probable user intent
- content is trustworthy and readable
- headings are too vague, duplicated, or thin
- important sections are missing
- keyword stuffing is avoided

### 5. Internal Linking

Check whether:

- related pages are linked where useful
- links support navigation and topic relationships
- conversion paths are supported
- internal links feel intentional, not random

### 6. URL and Structure Awareness

Review whether:

- the page slug is readable and descriptive
- content hierarchy supports crawlability
- SEO decisions are not being buried in random component code

## Output Format

When using this skill, return:

### SEO Findings
- clear issues found

### Recommended Changes
- specific structural improvements

### Priority
Classify each issue as:
- high
- medium
- low

### Acceptance Checklist
Provide a short checklist for implementation and QA

## Rules

- do not force awkward keywords
- do not damage readability for SEO
- do not recommend multiple H1s
- do not treat styling as heading structure
- do not ignore trust and clarity
- do not suggest changes that weaken medical credibility

## Good Use Cases

- audit a service page
- review H1/H2/H3 structure
- improve semantic content layout
- identify missing metadata
- suggest internal links
- review a landing page for SEO structure problems
